name: Franklin Delano Roosevelt
description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
abstract: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945. He is the longest-serving US president and the only one to have served more than two terms.

about_quotes.speaker: Ramsey Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee Ramsey Lewis does not mention Franklin Delano Roosevelt in his speech. FDR is only mentioned by the interviewer in a narrative context regarding Obama and A. Philip Randolph.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ramsey Lewis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hinton Milt
about_quotes.evidence: That was the convention that nominated Franklin Delano Roosevelt for President... I remember that was just so good because it was convention that nominated Franklin Delano Roosevelt.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hinton Milt — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: Up until then you’d see pictures of Franklin Delano Roosevelt with Dr. George Washington Carver; Eleanor Roosevelt with Marion Anderson, but they weren’t doing anything.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 16, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Johnson Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: But when the Volstead Act came along, that was prohibition you know, President Roosevelt, we tried to get a liquor license and we couldn’t get it
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Johnson Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: September found us rehearsing with a show that was to make a propaganda tour for the re-election of President Franklin D.Roosevelt.

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: When Franklin D. Roosevelt was running, and I thought he was the permanent President, he carried our ward, Ward 12, overwhelmingly
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
